top_categories.workout_context: At mile 60 of 100, glycogen is gone. This workout teaches your body to produce watts on fumes.
Race-pace efforts that mimic Hotter'N Hell Hundred's demands. Pacing, fueling, and tactical decisions under fatigue. Practice the race before August.
Every hard session works better with a bigger aerobic base. At 100 miles, base fitness is the difference between racing and surviving.
When someone attacks on flat to gently rolling North Texas plains — paved, you have 10 seconds to respond or you're off the back. This builds that response.
Hotter'N Hell Hundred's flat to gently rolling North Texas plains — paved forces constant power changes. Surge over the rough stuff, settle on the smooth, repeat for 100 miles.
100 miles rewards fat oxidation. This volume work shifts your fuel mix so you're still burning fat at hour 5, not bonking at hour 3.
Hotter'N Hell Hundred's flat to gently rolling North Texas plains — paved rewards steady threshold power. Surging and recovering costs more watts than just holding.
88–92% FTP is where you'll spend the hardest sustained miles of Hotter'N Hell Hundred. Train it until it feels boring.
Long rides with hard efforts mixed in. Simulates what Hotter'N Hell Hundred's flat to gently rolling North Texas plains — paved actually does to you.
2-minute all-out efforts on flat to gently rolling North Texas plains — paved. Closing gaps, responding to attacks, sprinting for position. Train the burn.
At Hotter'N Hell Hundred, surges push you above threshold and you have to recover while still riding. Over-unders train exactly that.
How long can you hold 105% FTP before you blow? At Hotter'N Hell Hundred, that number decides whether you bridge or get dropped.

race_overlay.heat: August in Wichita Falls, Texas, USA — The name says it all. Late August in Wichita Falls, Texas delivers average high temperatures of 97F with regular excursions past 100F and occasional spikes to 106-108F. The heat index — factoring in humidity — routinely exceeds 111F. Temperatures begin climbing past 100F as early as 9:45 AM, meaning 100-mile riders face 4-5 hours of triple-digit riding. August is the least humid month in Wichita Falls (average 50% relative humidity), but the dry heat is relentless and unshaded. Direct sun exposure on open prairie roads compounds the perceived temperature by 10-15F beyond the official heat index. This is North Texas — the wind never stops, but in August it blows hot, full of dust, and smells of baked asphalt and prairie. Begin heat acclimatization 10–14 days before Hotter'N Hell Hundred — 20–30min sauna sessions or midday rides in full kit. Pre-load sodium 48 hours out. Target 500–750ml/hr with electrolytes on race day. Race-day realities: Heat index exceeding 111F with direct sun exposure adding 10-15F; Dehydration and heat illness are serious medical risks — heat exhaustion and heatstroke send riders to medical tents every year.
race_overlay.nutrition: Target 60–80g carbs/hour for Hotter'N Hell Hundred’s 100 miles. Start fueling within the first 30 minutes — early fueling prevents late-race collapse. Bonking at mile 60 is a nutrition failure, not a fitness failure.

pack_summary: This 10-workout pack focuses on Durability, Race Simulation, and Endurance to prepare you for 100 miles of flat to gently rolling north texas plains — paved farm-to-market roads through wichita and clay counties in Wichita Falls, Texas, USA.
